frameworks.name: temporal context effect
frameworks.one_line: long-flash context monotonically alters perceived brightness of a short flash

frameworks.name: retinotopic source reconstruction
frameworks.one_line: MEG sources mapped to visual-area ROIs to dissociate brightness from luminance

frameworks.name: bottom-up vs top-down/lateral brightness coding
frameworks.one_line: fast stimulus-energy response vs slower context-driven integration

core_question: Which visual cortical areas and time windows encode context-dependent brightness versus physical luminance?
key_assumptions: perceived brightness can dissociate from physical luminance
MEG source reconstruction can isolate occipital ROIs
TCE arises from interaction between long-flash context and short flash
manipulations: onset asynchrony between short flash and constant long-flash patch
physical luminance level of short flash
key_findings: TCE-related responses in LO and V4 at 200-250 ms
luminance-driven responses in V1/V2, V4 and VO around 100 ms
different visual areas show differential representation of onset vs offset synchronized flashes
interpretations: two processes underlie brightness coding: fast bottom-up energy and slower context-driven top-down/lateral
V4 may dynamically integrate luminance signals into brightness perception
